member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Archival.AtomicFileWriter
member.summary: Provides atomic file write operations using write-to-temp-then-rename pattern. Ensures files are never partially written even on crash or power loss.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.AtomicFileWriter.WriteAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Atomically writes content to a file. Uses a temporary file with rename to ensure atomicity.

member.summary: Atomically writes binary content to a file.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.AtomicFileWriter.WriteAsync(System.String,System.Func{System.IO.StreamWriter,System.Threading.Tasks.Task},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Atomically writes content using a stream writer action.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.AtomicFileWriter.AppendAsync(System.String,System.Func{System.IO.Stream,System.Threading.Tasks.Task},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Atomically appends binary content to an existing file using copy-on-write semantics. The original file is preserved until the new file containing both the original and appended bytes has been fully written and renamed into place.

member.summary: Atomically appends UTF-8 lines to an existing text file using copy-on-write semantics.

member.summary: Atomically writes content with checksum verification.

member.summary: Verify a file's checksum matches its sidecar file.

member.summary: Atomically replace a file using copy-on-write semantics. Keeps a backup of the original.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog
member.summary: Write-Ahead Log (WAL) for durable, crash-safe storage operations. All market events are first written to the WAL before being committed to primary storage.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.LastRecoveryEventCount
member.summary: Gets the number of valid events recovered during the last initialization.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.LastRecoveryDurationMs
member.summary: Gets the duration of the last recovery in milliseconds.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.CorruptedRecordCount
member.summary: Gets the total number of corrupted records encountered across all reads and recoveries.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.SkippedRecordCount
member.summary: Gets the total number of records skipped due to corruption across all reads and recoveries.

member.name: E:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.CorruptionDetected
member.summary: Raised when a corrupted WAL record is detected during recovery, provided is . The argument is the number of corrupted records found in the current recovery pass. Subscribe to this event to forward alerts to your monitoring infrastructure.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.InitializeAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Initialize the WAL, recovering any uncommitted transactions.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.AppendAsync``1(``0,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Append a record to the WAL.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.CommitAsync(System.Int64,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Commit a batch of records, marking them as successfully persisted.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.FlushAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Flush any buffered writes to disk.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.FlushInternalAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Internal flush that assumes the caller already holds . Called from and which acquire the lock before invoking flush, avoiding a deadlock on the non-reentrant .

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.GetUncommittedRecordsAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Get uncommitted records for replay/recovery using streaming reads. Processes records in batches to avoid loading entire WAL into memory.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.TruncateAsync(System.Int64,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Truncate WAL files that have been fully committed.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.ComputeChecksum(System.Int64,System.DateTime,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Computes a SHA-256 checksum for a WAL record using incremental hashing to avoid allocating a single large concatenated string.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.RepairAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Repairs all WAL files by scanning every record, validating checksums, and rewriting only valid records into new WAL files. Corrupted records are discarded and counted in the result.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.ComputeChecksumForBenchmark(System.Int64,System.DateTime,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Exposes the private ComputeChecksum method to benchmark and test assemblies so they can measure the cost of checksum computation in isolation.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WriteAheadLog.WarmChecksumPath
member.summary: Pre-JITs the hot checksum path so that allocation-measurement tests (and the first production call) receive clean, zero-allocation baselines. Idempotent: only runs once per process lifetime.
member.remarks: The medium-payload warm-up (900 chars) specifically pre-initialises the SIMD UTF-8 encoding path that .NET 9 uses for strings longer than ~64 characters; without it, the first call allocates ~120 bytes of lazy-init state that inflates allocation-budget tests.

member.summary: A record in the Write-Ahead Log.

member.summary: WAL configuration options.

member.summary: Maximum WAL file size before rotation.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalOptions.MaxWalFileAge

member.summary: Sync mode for durability.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalOptions.SyncBatchSize
member.summary: Number of records to batch before syncing.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalOptions.MaxFlushDelay
member.summary: Maximum delay between flushes.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalOptions.ArchiveAfterTruncate
member.summary: Whether to archive WAL files after truncation.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalOptions.UncommittedSizeWarningThreshold
member.summary: Size threshold (bytes) at which a warning is logged about uncommitted WAL data. Default is 50MB.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalOptions.CorruptionMode
member.summary: Controls how the WAL behaves when corrupted records are detected during recovery. Defaults to to preserve backwards compatibility. Set to in production so monitoring systems are notified, or to require manual operator review.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalSyncMode
member.summary: WAL synchronization modes.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalSyncMode.NoSync
member.summary: No explicit sync - relies on OS buffering (fastest, least durable).

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalSyncMode.BatchedSync
member.summary: Sync after batches of writes (balanced).

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalSyncMode.EveryWrite
member.summary: Sync after every write (slowest, most durable).

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalCorruptionMode
member.summary: Controls how the WAL responds when corrupted records are detected during recovery.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalCorruptionMode.Skip
member.summary: Silently skip corrupt records and continue recovery (legacy behaviour).

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalCorruptionMode.Alert
member.summary: Skip corrupt records but fire the event so monitoring systems can alert operators. Recommended for production deployments.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Archival.WalCorruptionMode.Halt
member.summary: Throw an when any corrupt record is found. Use this when data integrity is non-negotiable and manual operator review is required before the application can start.

member.summary: Result of a WAL repair operation.

member.summary: Result of a WAL repair operation.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Export.AnalysisExportService
member.summary: Service for exporting collected market data in analysis-ready formats. Split into partial classes: Formats (CSV/Parquet/JSONL/Lean/SQL/XLSX), IO (documentation/loaders/utilities).

Feature engineering for exported data. Computes returns, rolling statistics, and technical indicators based on . Enriches raw record dictionaries with computed columns before writing to output formats.
Apache Arrow IPC (Feather v2) format export and type handling.
Simple format export methods (CSV, JSONL, Lean, SQL). Complex formats are in separate partials: Parquet, Xlsx, Arrow.
Parquet format export and type conversion helpers.
XLSX (Excel) format export with Open XML generation.
Documentation generation, loader scripts, and I/O utility methods.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Packaging.PortableDataPackager
member.summary: Package creation, file scanning, manifest building, and archive writing methods.

Service for creating and importing portable data packages. Packages include all data, metadata, quality reports, and scripts needed for data portability and sharing. Split into partial classes: Creation, Validation, Scripts.
Documentation generation and data loader script methods. Database import scripts are in Scripts.Sql.cs and Scripts.Import.cs.
Language-specific database import script generation (Python, R, Spark).
Database import script generation (PostgreSQL, ClickHouse, DuckDB).
Package validation, manifest reading, import/extraction, and utility methods.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1
member.summary:
Thread-safe generic event buffer using a swap-buffer drain strategy to eliminate per-drain allocations. Maintains two instances: _active (receives new events) and _standby (returned to callers from so they can process events without holding the lock).
Eliminates the duplicate lock-based buffer implementations that existed in JsonlStorageSink and ParquetStorageSink.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1._lock
member.summary: Lock protecting and .

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1._active
member.summary: Active list that receives new events. Swapped with on drain. Accessible to subclasses for single-lock operations such as per-symbol partitioning.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1._count
member.summary: Approximate count of buffered events. Updated inside but readable without the lock (volatile) so that and avoid lock acquisition on every incoming event.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new event buffer.
member.param: Initial backing-list capacity hint (default: 1000).
Maximum number of events to retain. When the limit is reached the oldest event is silently dropped to make room for the new one (drop-oldest policy). Defaults to (unbounded).

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.Count
member.summary: Gets the approximate current count of buffered events. This is a lock-free volatile read; callers should tolerate minor staleness.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.IsEmpty
member.summary: Gets whether the buffer is empty (lock-free).

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.ShouldFlush(System.Int32)
member.summary: Returns when the buffered event count meets or exceeds . Lock-free — safe to call on every incoming event.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.Add(`0)
member.summary: Add a single event to the buffer. When the buffer is at maxCapacity the oldest buffered event is dropped to make room (drop-oldest policy).

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.AddRange(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{`0})
member.summary: Add multiple events to the buffer, applying the drop-oldest policy as needed.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.DrainAll
member.summary:
Atomically drains all buffered events via a buffer swap — no copy allocation.
The returned is the buffer's internal backing store and will be cleared and reused on the next call to . Callers must complete all processing of the returned list before the next drain cycle begins on the same buffer instance.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.Drain(System.Int32)
member.summary: Drain up to events from the front of the buffer.
member.remarks: This operation is O(n) in the number of remaining elements because must shift the tail. For high-frequency partial drains consider using with an external cap instead.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.PeekAll
member.summary: Returns a snapshot of all buffered events without removing them. Allocates a new list on every call; intended for diagnostics/monitoring only.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.EventBuffer`1.Clear
member.summary: Clear all events from the buffer.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Services.MarketEventBuffer
member.summary: Specialized event buffer for types.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.MarketEventBuffer.DrainBySymbol(System.String)
member.summary: Drain events for a specific symbol in a single lock acquisition. Non-matching events remain in the buffer and preserve their original ordering.
member.remarks: The previous implementation called followed by in two separate lock acquisitions, creating a race window where concurrently produced events could be silently reordered. This implementation partitions events in a single pass under one lock.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Services.FileMaintenanceService
member.summary: Service for file health monitoring, self-healing, and maintenance operations.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Services.FilePermissionsService
member.summary:
Cross-platform service for managing file and directory permissions. Ensures the data directory is properly access-restricted for security.
Unix/Linux: Sets 0755 for directories (rwxr-xr-x), 0644 for files (rw-r--r--) Windows: Configures NTFS ACLs to restrict access to current user + administrators

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Services.JsonlPositionSnapshotStore
member.summary:
JSONL-backed implementation of .
Path convention: {StorageRoot}/portfolios/{runId}/{accountId}/snapshots.jsonl This path falls under StorageOptions.RootPath so the automatically picks it up for tiered-storage lifecycle enforcement (ADR-002). Each snapshot is a single JSON line appended atomically (ADR-007) — position snapshots are idempotent (latest wins), so recovery simply reads to the last successfully written line.

member.summary: Streams lines from a file in forward (oldest-first) order.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Services.JsonlPositionSnapshotStore.ReadLinesReverseAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Streams lines from a file in reverse (newest-first) order by reading blocks from the end.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.CompositeSink
member.summary: Fans out events to multiple storage sinks, enabling multi-format storage (e.g., JSONL + Parquet simultaneously) without modifying the EventPipeline. Includes per-sink circuit breaker health tracking to avoid hammering a persistently failing sink.

member.summary: Per-sink mutable health tracker. Indexed by sink position in . Access is synchronised via and atomic field updates on the tracker instances.

member.summary: Gets the number of sinks currently in the state.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.JsonlBatchOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for batched JSONL storage.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.JsonlBatchOptions.BatchSize
member.summary: Number of events to buffer before writing to disk. Default is 1000 events.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.JsonlBatchOptions.FlushInterval
member.summary: Maximum time between flushes. Default is 5 seconds.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.JsonlBatchOptions.Enabled
member.summary: Whether batching is enabled. When disabled, writes occur immediately per event. Default is true.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.JsonlBatchOptions.ParallelSerializationThreshold
member.summary: Pre-serialize events in parallel when batch size exceeds this threshold. Default is 5000 events — below this, the context-switching overhead of Parallel.For exceeds the serialization savings.

member.summary: Default options with batching enabled.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.JsonlBatchOptions.HighThroughput
member.summary: Optimized for high throughput with larger batches.

member.summary: Optimized for low latency with smaller batches.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.JsonlBatchOptions.NoBatching
member.summary: Disable batching - write each event immediately.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Sinks.ParquetStorageSink

Apache Parquet storage sink for high-performance columnar storage. Provides 10-20x better compression than JSONL and optimized for analytics.
Based on: https://github.com/aloneguid/parquet-dotnet (MIT) Reference: docs/open-source-references.md #20

member.summary: Writes Parquet data atomically using a temp-file-then-rename strategy. Prevents partially written files from appearing at the destination on crash or I/O error.

member.summary: Custom file name prefix (optional).

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.RetentionDays
member.summary: Optional retention window (in days). Older files are deleted eagerly when new data arrives.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.MaxTotalBytes
member.summary: Optional maximum on-disk footprint in bytes. When exceeded, the oldest files are deleted first.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.Tiering
member.summary: Storage tier configuration for tiered storage architecture.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.Quotas
member.summary: Quota configuration for capacity management.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.Policies
member.summary: Storage policy configuration by event type.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.GenerateManifests
member.summary: Whether to generate file manifests for catalog indexing.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.EmbedChecksum
member.summary: Whether to embed checksum in file names.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.VerifyOnRead
member.summary: Whether to verify per-record checksums when reading data files. When true, each record read from JSONL/WAL storage is validated against its stored checksum; corrupted records are skipped and logged. Incurs a small CPU overhead on read paths. Default is false.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.EnableParquetSink
member.summary: Whether to enable Parquet storage as an additional sink alongside JSONL. When enabled, events are written to both JSONL and Parquet via CompositeSink.
member.remarks: Superseded by when that list is non-empty. Retained for backward compatibility.

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.ActiveSinks
member.summary: Explicit list of storage sink plugin IDs to activate at startup. When non-empty, overrides and drives dynamic composition via StorageSinkRegistry.
member.remarks: Each entry must match the Id declared on a discovered -decorated class. Example: ["jsonl", "parquet"]

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageOptions.PartitionStrategy
member.summary: Partition strategy for multi-dimensional partitioning.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention
member.summary: File naming and directory structure conventions.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention.Flat
member.summary: Flat structure: {root}/{symbol}_{type}_{date}.jsonl All files in root directory, good for small datasets. Example: data/AAPL_Trade_2024-01-15.jsonl

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention.BySymbol
member.summary: Organize by symbol first: {root}/{symbol}/{type}/{date}.jsonl Best when analyzing individual symbols over time. Example: data/AAPL/Trade/2024-01-15.jsonl

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention.ByDate
member.summary: Organize by date first: {root}/{date}/{symbol}/{type}.jsonl Best for daily batch processing and archival. Example: data/2024-01-15/AAPL/Trade.jsonl

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention.ByType
member.summary: Organize by event type first: {root}/{type}/{symbol}/{date}.jsonl Best when analyzing specific event types across symbols. Example: data/Trade/AAPL/2024-01-15.jsonl

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention.BySource
member.summary: Organize by data source: {root}/{source}/{symbol}/{type}/{date}.jsonl Best when comparing data across multiple providers. Example: data/alpaca/AAPL/Trade/2024-01-15.jsonl

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention.ByAssetClass
member.summary: Organize by asset class: {root}/{asset_class}/{symbol}/{type}/{date}.jsonl Best when managing multiple asset classes. Example: data/equity/AAPL/Trade/2024-01-15.jsonl

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention.Hierarchical
member.summary: Full hierarchical taxonomy: {root}/{source}/{asset_class}/{symbol}/{type}/{date}.jsonl Best for enterprise multi-source, multi-asset deployments. Example: data/alpaca/equity/AAPL/Trade/2024-01-15.jsonl

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.FileNamingConvention.Canonical
member.summary: Canonical time-based: {root}/{year}/{month}/{day}/{source}/{symbol}/{type}.jsonl Best for time-series analysis and archival. Example: data/2024/01/15/alpaca/AAPL/Trade.jsonl

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.DatePartition
member.summary: Date-based file partitioning strategy.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.DatePartition.None
member.summary: No date partitioning - all data in single file per symbol/type. File name: {symbol}_{type}.jsonl

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.DatePartition.Daily
member.summary: Partition by day: {date:yyyy-MM-dd}

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.DatePartition.Hourly
member.summary: Partition by hour: {date:yyyy-MM-dd_HH} Good for high-volume data.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.DatePartition.Monthly
member.summary: Partition by month: {date:yyyy-MM} Good for long-term storage with less granularity.

member.summary: LZ4 - fast compression, lower ratio.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.CompressionCodec.Gzip
member.summary: Gzip - balanced compression.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.CompressionCodec.Zstd
member.summary: Zstd - high compression ratio.

member.name: F:Meridian.Storage.CompressionCodec.Brotli
member.summary: Brotli - high compression, slower.

member.summary: Critical data - never delete (regulatory/compliance).

member.summary: Registry for discovering and registering plugins that are decorated with .

member.name: P:Meridian.Storage.StorageSinkRegistry.Sinks
member.summary: Gets the discovered storage sink metadata entries, in the order they were found.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.StorageSinkRegistry.DiscoverFromAssemblies(System.Reflection.Assembly[])
member.summary: Scans the provided assemblies for concrete types decorated with that implement , and adds their metadata to the registry.
member.remarks: Duplicate IDs (case-insensitive) are silently ignored so that scanning the same assembly twice is safe.
member.param: One or more assemblies to scan.
member.exception: Thrown when no assemblies are supplied.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.StorageSinkRegistry.RegisterServices(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.ServiceLifetime)
member.summary: Registers all discovered sink implementation types into the service collection so that they can be resolved by during composition.
member.remarks: Only registers types that are not already present in the collection, so calling this method after explicit registrations is safe.
member.param: The DI service collection to populate.
Service lifetime for registered sinks. Defaults to singleton.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.StorageSinkRegistry.TryGetSink(System.String,Meridian.Storage.StorageSinkMetadata@)
member.summary: Attempts to find a registered sink by its identifier (case-insensitive).
member.param: The sink identifier to look up (e.g., "jsonl", "parquet").
When this method returns true, contains the sink metadata; otherwise null.
member.returns: true if a matching sink was found; otherwise false.

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Store.CompositeMarketDataStore
member.summary: that aggregates results from multiple underlying stores and merges them in ascending order.

remarks.para: Use this as the top-level store when data lives across different tiers or formats (e.g. JSONL hot tier + Parquet cold tier). Each delegate store is queried independently; their results are merged via an N-way sorted merge so callers always receive a coherent time-ordered stream.
The is applied after merging, so all delegates are queried until the combined limit is reached.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Store.CompositeMarketDataStore.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Meridian.Storage.Interfaces.IMarketDataStore})
member.summary: Initialises a composite store that fans out queries to and merges results by timestamp.
member.param: One or more backing stores to query.

member.name: M:Meridian.Storage.Store.CompositeMarketDataStore.#ctor(Meridian.Storage.Interfaces.IMarketDataStore,Meridian.Storage.Interfaces.IMarketDataStore)
member.summary: Convenience constructor for two stores (e.g. JSONL + Parquet).

member.name: T:Meridian.Storage.Store.JsonlMarketDataStore
member.summary: implementation backed by JSONL (optionally gzip-compressed) files. Enumerates all *.jsonl and *.jsonl.gz files under the configured root, deserialises each line, and applies the predicate in-process.
